description:
Goal
Make provider-boundary Process.heap_alloc_stats/0 values flow through compiler2 without recursive overflow, so runtime refs and heap-stats fixtures can compile normally.

Minimal repros
- fn main() do
    stats = Process.heap_alloc_stats()
    dbg(stats)
  end
- fn main() do
    stats = Process.heap_alloc_stats()
    dbg(stats[:list_cons_allocs])
  end
- fixtures2/00007_runtime_refs.fz
- fixtures2/00297_heap_alloc_stats.fz

Observed facts
- Process.heap_alloc_stats() by itself compiles.
- dbg(%{list_cons_allocs: 1, map_allocs: 2}[:list_cons_allocs]) compiles.
- The overflow requires a value flowing from Process.heap_alloc_stats/0 into dbg or dbg after map projection from that provider result.
- Reproduces on exported HEAD at 2ec9d07dd (fz-hwn.3), so this is not introduced by the uncommitted fz-hwn.4 callable-entry work.

Why this matters
This is a separate modeling/runtime-refs bug. It should not be “fixed” by widening callable-demand work or by reintroducing type-driven callable inventory. The right fix is to understand the provider-boundary any/map value flow and make the relevant demand/ABI path terminate and stay value-shaped.

Code seams to inspect
- src/modules/runtime_library/process.fz
- src/compiler2/jobs/root.rs via fixtures2/00007_runtime_refs.fz
- src/compiler2/jobs/runtime_demand.rs provider-boundary return and structural reverse-propagation
- src/compiler2/jobs/artifact.rs runtime param / ABI derivation for provider-returned values
- src/compiler2 lowering for Kernel.dbg and Process.heap_alloc_stats roots

Acceptance signal
- compiler2_runtime_refs_pull_only_the_reached_runtime_modules resolves
- process_heap_alloc_stats_returns_allocation_map resolves
- minimal repros above compile without stack overflow
- no new callable-entry or callable-boundary inventory appears for these value-only paths
title: Compiler2 runtime refs: Process.heap_alloc_stats dbg path stack-overflows during compilation
